children.name: Mortality
Mortality
Mortality
Crude mortality rate
Deaths per 10,000 per day

metric.label: Deaths per 10,000 per day
metric.msna_indicator: # of deaths per 10,000 people per day and / or # of under-5 year old deaths per 10,000 under-5 children per day
metric.remarks_limitations: Need denominator
metric.references_for_threshold: Well-established emergency threshold (WHO, Shpere, UNHCR Emergency Handbook) https://emergency.unhcr.org/emergency-assistance/health-and-nutrition/mortality-surveillance-threshold

children.name: U5 mortality rate
Deaths of under fives per 10,000 per day

metric.label: Deaths of under fives per 10,000 per day
metric.msna_indicator: # of deaths per 10,000 people per day and / or # of under-5 year old deaths per 10,000 under-5 children per day
metric.remarks_limitations: Need denominator
metric.references_for_threshold: Well-established emergency threshold (WHO, Shpere, UNHCR Emergency Handbook) https://emergency.unhcr.org/emergency-assistance/health-and-nutrition/mortality-surveillance-threshold

children.name: Health outcomes
Nutrition status
Acute Malnutrition
Incidence of acute malnutrition
% of children 6-59 months GAM by WHZ

metric.label: % of children 6-59 months GAM by WHZ
metric.remarks_limitations: If data covers all chidlren 0-59 months, value would slightly over-estimate acute malnutrition, as children 0-6 months are usually be breastfed and are likely to be flagged as acuteley malnourished by WHO standards. Extra caution is advised.
metric.references_for_threshold: Recommended threshold according to IPC Acute Malnutrition (2018). GAM 15-29.9% refers to IPC AMN Critical phase (4).

children.name: % of children 6-59 months GAM by MUAC

metric.label: % of children 6-59 months GAM by MUAC
metric.remarks_limitations: Prevalence of GAM by MUAC should be referred to as “proxy” GAM, as it is not a true representative indicator of malnutrition in the population. This is because MUAC measurements are more sensitive younger children (under 2 years of age) and will likely not detect older children that are malnourished by the WHZ criteria.
metric.references_for_threshold: IPC Guidance suggests usign MUAC only when WHZ is not available. 10-15% GAM by MUAC corresponds roughly to IPC AMN Phase 3 and 4. Acute Needs threshold is therefore slightly conservative.

children.name: % of children 6-59 months combined GAM

metric.label: % of children 6-59 months combined GAM

children.name: IPC AMN Classification
IPC AMN Phase

metric.label: IPC AMN Phase
metric.references_for_threshold: IPC AMN Classification (Phase 4 = Critical, Phase 5 = Extremely Critical)

children.name: Change in acute malnutrition cases
% change of monthly new SAM admissions at Stabilization Center or OTP programs

metric.label: % change of monthly new SAM admissions at Stabilization Center or OTP programs

children.name: % change of monthly defaulters to SC or OTP programs

metric.label: % change of monthly defaulters to SC or OTP programs

children.name: % change of monthly deaths reported in Stabilization Centers or OTP

metric.label: % change of monthly deaths reported in Stabilization Centers or OTP

children.name: Trends of acute malnutriton in the last 3 months
Trends of acute malnutriton in the last 3 months

metric.label: Trends of acute malnutriton in the last 3 months

children.name: Stock-outs of RUTF/ RUSF nutrition supplements/products in the last 3 months
Qualitative reports of stock-outs of RUTF/ RUSF nutrition supplements/products in the last 3 months (% stock-out)

metric.label: Qualitative reports of stock-outs of RUTF/ RUSF nutrition supplements/products in the last 3 months (% stock-out)

children.name: Number of community outreach activities, refferals and community screening of malnutrition by community health workers in catchment area in last 1 month.
Qualitative reports of number of community outreach, referrals and community screenings of malnutrition in last 1 month

metric.label: Qualitative reports of number of community outreach, referrals and community screenings of malnutrition in last 1 month

children.name: Health status
Common childhood illnesses
Incidence of common childhood illnesses and symptoms per epiweek
# of cases (all-causes) of common childhood illness in children under-5 years of age per 1000, per epiweek

metric.label: # of cases (all-causes) of common childhood illness in children under-5 years of age per 1000, per epiweek

children.name: # of cases of acute respiratory illness in children under-5 years of age per 1000, per epiweek

metric.label: # of cases of acute respiratory illness in children under-5 years of age per 1000, per epiweek

children.name: # of cases of acute watery diarrhoea in children under-5 years of age per 1000, per epiweek

metric.label: # of cases of acute watery diarrhoea in children under-5 years of age per 1000, per epiweek

children.name: Two-week prevalence of childhood illness
% of children under-5 years with any illness in the two weeks prior to data collection

metric.label: % of children under-5 years with any illness in the two weeks prior to data collection
metric.msna_indicator: % of children under-5 years of age sick in the last two weeks as reported by primary caregiver
metric.references_for_threshold: ANA analytical threshold, to be interpreted against seasonality, baseline incidence, surveillance quality, and triangulated evidence of service strain or outbreak escalation.

children.name: % of children under-5 years with any acute respiratory infection in the two weeks prior to data collection

metric.label: % of children under-5 years with any acute respiratory infection in the two weeks prior to data collection
metric.msna_indicator: % of children under-5 years of age sick in the last two weeks as reported by primary caregiver, by symptom
metric.remarks_limitations: Not confirmed but can obtain symptomology
metric.references_for_threshold: ANA analytical threshold, to be interpreted against seasonality, baseline incidence, surveillance quality, and triangulated evidence of service strain or outbreak escalation.

children.name: % of children under-5 years with any acute watery diarrhoea in the two weeks prior to data collection

metric.label: % of children under-5 years with any acute watery diarrhoea in the two weeks prior to data collection
metric.msna_indicator: % of children under-5 years of age sick in the last two weeks as reported by primary caregiver, by symptom
metric.remarks_limitations: Not confirmed but can obtain symptomology
metric.references_for_threshold: ANA analytical threshold, to be interpreted against seasonality, baseline incidence, surveillance quality, and triangulated evidence of service strain or outbreak escalation.

children.name: Infectious disease outbreaks
Evidence of measles outbreaks
# of confirmed or suspected cases of measles in children 6 months to 15 years of age (or context specific age groups), where at least one is confirmed

metric.label: # of confirmed or suspected cases of measles in children 6 months to 15 years of age (or context specific age groups), where at least one is confirmed
metric.references_for_threshold: WHO defines suspected measles outbreak as five or more epidemiologically linked cases, and confirmed outbreak as two or more lab-confirmed linked cases. This threshold is adapted for the ANA to flag concern.  https://www.who.int/emergencies/outbreak-toolkit/disease-outbreak-toolboxes/measles-outbreak-toolbox

children.name: # of cases of measles in children 6 months to 15 years of age per 1000 children, per epiweek

metric.label: # of cases of measles in children 6 months to 15 years of age per 1000 children, per epiweek
metric.remarks_limitations: At this level, measles transmission is likely widespread, with high exposure among susceptible children and increased risk of complications and excess mortality in contexts with malnutrition or weak health systems.
metric.references_for_threshold:
ANA analytical threshold in the absence of an overall global standard threshold. Based on the Sphere Handbook, WHO outbreak response guidelines, MSF field thresholds 
https://medicalguidelines.msf.org/sites/default/files/pdf/guideline-498-en.pdf

children.name: Reports of measles outbreaks from disease surveillance systems, rapid assessments, or other secondary sources
Confirmed measles outbreak, not controlled (cases increasing weekly, see epidemic curve)

metric.label: Confirmed measles outbreak, not controlled (cases increasing weekly, see epidemic curve)
metric.references_for_threshold: ANA analytical threshold, binary threshold is analytical rather than global severity cut-off. See WHO disease-specific toolboxes https://www.who.int/emergencies/outbreak-toolkit/disease-outbreak-toolboxes

children.name: Evidence of cholera outbreaks
# of cases of cholera/suspect cholera in populations where at least one case is confirmed

metric.label: # of cases of cholera/suspect cholera in populations where at least one case is confirmed
metric.references_for_threshold: WHO/GTFCC define a suspected cholera outbreak when two or more suspected cholera cases or one suspected case with positive RDT result are reported within one surveillance unit within 7 days. This threshold is adapted for the ANA to flag outbreak severity. https://www.gtfcc.org/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/gtfcc-cholera-outbreak-response-field-manual-2024.pdf

children.name: # of cases of cholera/suspect cholera per 1000 people per week (all ages)

metric.label: # of cases of cholera/suspect cholera per 1000 people per week (all ages)
metric.references_for_threshold: ANA analytical threshold in the absence of an overall global standard threshold. Based on the Sphere Handbook, WHO outbreak response guidelines, MSF field thresholds

children.name: Reports of cholera outbreaks from disease surveillance systems, rapid assessments, or other secondary sources
Confirmed outbreak, not controlled (cases increasing weekly, see epidemic curve)

metric.label: Confirmed outbreak, not controlled (cases increasing weekly, see epidemic curve)

children.name: Reports of possible spikes in infectious diseases e.g. from expert health KII
Reports of large increases in malaria, diarrhoeal disease, respiratory infectionsor other infectious disease not attributable to pre-crisis conditions or seasonality

metric.label: Reports of large increases in malaria, diarrhoeal disease, respiratory infectionsor other infectious disease not attributable to pre-crisis conditions or seasonality

children.name: Reports of suspected measles outbreak e.g. from expert health KII
Qualitative reports of children with cough + neck rashes + fevers as suspect measles cases

metric.label: Qualitative reports of children with cough + neck rashes + fevers as suspect measles cases

children.name: Reports of suspected cholera outbreak e.g. from expert health KII
Qualitative reports of adults with serious cases of acute watery diarrhoea

metric.label: Qualitative reports of adults with serious cases of acute watery diarrhoea

children.name: Reports of increasing health needs e.g. from expert health KII

Qualitative reports of an increase in health needs in the community (e.g. overcrowded health facilities, community events or normal activities of the community disrupted due to illnesses, increased number of deaths in the community, etc.)

metric.label: Qualitative reports of an increase in health needs in the community (e.g. overcrowded health facilities, community events or normal activities of the community disrupted due to illnesses, increased number of deaths in the community, etc.)

children.name: Population health needs
Overall infectious disease incidence (all cause and all age)
# of consulations/treatments for infectious diseases per 1000 people per epiweek

metric.label: # of consulations/treatments for infectious diseases per 1000 people per epiweek

children.name: Any unmet health needs in past three months
% of individuals with unmet health needs in the past 3 months

metric.label: % of individuals with unmet health needs in the past 3 months
metric.msna_indicator: % of individuals with an unmet healthcare need

children.name: Any unmet health needs for life-saving services in past three months

% of inviduals who reported needing consultation or drugs for acute illness, consultation or drugs for chronic illness, trauma care, emergency surgery, ante-natal or post-natal services, and/or safe-delivery services and were not able to access it

metric.label: % of inviduals who reported needing consultation or drugs for acute illness, consultation or drugs for chronic illness, trauma care, emergency surgery, ante-natal or post-natal services, and/or safe-delivery services and were not able to access it
metric.msna_indicator: % of individuals with a healthcare need, by type of healthcare

children.name: Food system
Food consumption
Quantity
Minimum Meal Frequency (MMF) for children 6-23 months
Breastfed (6-8 months) => 2 times Breastfed (9-23 months) => 3 times Not breastfed (6-23 months) => 4 times

metric.label: Breastfed (6-8 months) => 2 times Breastfed (9-23 months) => 3 times Not breastfed (6-23 months) => 4 times
metric.references_for_threshold: Based on WHO/UNICEF IYCF minimum standards; no global prevalence threshold exists. ANA threshold reflects a high prevalence of inadequate feeding practices.

children.name: Diversity
Minimum Dietary Diversity (MDD) for children 6-23 months
consumption of4 out of 8 food groups (for breastfed) or â¥5 (for non-breastfed children, including milk feeds)

metric.label: consumption of4 out of 8 food groups (for breastfed) or â¥5 (for non-breastfed children, including milk feeds)
metric.references_for_threshold: Based on WHO/UNICEF IYCF minimum standards; no global prevalence threshold exists. ANA threshold reflects a high prevalence of inadequate feeding practices.

children.name: Minimal dietary diversity for women (MDD-W)
MDD-W => 5 food groups at least

metric.label: MDD-W => 5 food groups at least
metric.references_for_threshold:
Aligned with FAO-MDD-W guidelines (standard is ≥5 of 10 food groups)
https://www.fao.org/nutrition/assessment/tools/minimum-dietary-diversity-women/en/

children.name: Quantity and diversity (proxy)
IPC AFI classification
IPC AFI phase

metric.label: IPC AFI phase
metric.remarks_limitations: FEWSNET phases, or Cadre Harmonisé phases can also be used as equivalent
metric.references_for_threshold: IPC AFI Classification (Phase 4 = Emergency, Phase 5 = Castarophe/Famine)

children.name: CARI
% of people in CARI P4

metric.label: % of people in CARI P4
metric.references_for_threshold: Based on WFP CARI classification; threshold adapted to reflect a substantial share of the population in severe food insecurity (Phase 4), aligned with 20% rule from the IPC Guidelines.

children.name: Household hunger scale
% of HHs reporting severe or very severe hunger

metric.label: % of HHs reporting severe or very severe hunger
metric.msna_indicator: Household Hunger Scale
metric.remarks_limitations: Can use PHU4IMPACT functions to compute the composite scores
metric.references_for_threshold: ANA analytical threshold for area-level flagging, based on IPC AFI Reference table: HHS 4 corresponds with IPC AFI Phase 4 (Emergency), HHS 5-6 corresponds with IPC AFI Phase 5 (Catastrophe/Famine)

children.name: % of HHs reporting very severe hunger

metric.label: % of HHs reporting very severe hunger
metric.msna_indicator: Household Hunger Scale
metric.remarks_limitations: Can use PHU4IMPACT functions to compute the composite scores
metric.references_for_threshold: ANA analytical threshold for area-level flagging, based on IPC AFI Reference table: HHS 4 corresponds with IPC AFI Phase 4 (Emergency), HHS 5-6 corresponds with IPC AFI Phase 5 (Catastrophe/Famine)

children.name: % of HHs reporting high consumption-based coping

metric.label: % of HHs reporting high consumption-based coping
metric.msna_indicator: Reduced Coping Strategies Index
metric.remarks_limitations: Can use PHU4IMPACT functions to compute the composite scores
metric.references_for_threshold: ANA analytical threshold for area-level flagging. WFP typical benchmark / IPC reference table lists an rCSI over 19 as crisis/emergency conditions. Value here is reflecting high proportion of population engaging in high coping.

children.name: FCS
% of HHs scoring poor FCS score

metric.label: % of HHs scoring poor FCS score
metric.msna_indicator: Food Consumption Score
metric.remarks_limitations: Can use PHU4IMPACT functions to compute the composite scores
metric.references_for_threshold: ANA analytical threshold for area-level flagging. WFP Standard threshold for poor FCS is ≤21. Value of 20% aligned with "20% rule" from the IPC guidelines.

children.name: Experience of hunger by community (AoK)
% of localities experiencing severe or extreme hunger

metric.label: % of localities experiencing severe or extreme hunger
metric.references_for_threshold: No global threshold exists. ANA analytical hreshold based on alignment with IPC severity logic.

children.name: Qualitative reports of extreme hunger

Qualitative reports of people (reasonable share per unit of analysis) not eating for a few days (2-4 days) visible due to overall catastrophic conditions within the unit of analysis. Other context-specific indications can include: people tying a rope or piece of cloth around their waist to stop hunger pangs (South Sudan), extreme reliance on wild foods.

metric.label: Qualitative reports of people (reasonable share per unit of analysis) not eating for a few days (2-4 days) visible due to overall catastrophic conditions within the unit of analysis. Other context-specific indications can include: people tying a rope or piece of cloth around their waist to stop hunger pangs (South Sudan), extreme reliance on wild foods.
metric.references_for_threshold: No global threshold exists. ANA analytical hreshold based on alignment with IPC severity logic.

children.name: Qualitative reports of mainly children eating
Qualitative reports indicating that adults within a household are skipping meals or reducing their food consumption to favor children's or pregnant and lactating women's food consumption

metric.label: Qualitative reports indicating that adults within a household are skipping meals or reducing their food consumption to favor children's or pregnant and lactating women's food consumption
metric.references_for_threshold: No global threshold exists. ANA analytical hreshold based on alignment with IPC severity logic.

children.name: Household Dietary Diversity Score (HDDS)
% of HHS reporting a low dietary diversity score

metric.label: % of HHS reporting a low dietary diversity score
metric.msna_indicator: Household Food Consumption (HDDS)
metric.references_for_threshold: HDDS of 0-2 food groups aligns with IPC AFI Phase 4 or 5. No universally agreed global threshold for % of HHs, this ANA analytical threshold is based on alignment with IPC severity logic, 40% indicating a widespread issue.

children.name: Quantity (proxy)
Average number of meals
% of HHs relying on one meal per day

metric.label: % of HHs relying on one meal per day
metric.references_for_threshold: No global threshold exists. ANA analytical hreshold based on alignment with IPC severity logic.

children.name: HH food security
Availability
Decrease in HH food stocks
% HHs with significant decrease in average # of months of food stocks

metric.label: % HHs with significant decrease in average # of months of food stocks

children.name: Availability of monitored staple cereals in the market
# of monitored staple cereals for which limited availability or no availability is reported

metric.label: # of monitored staple cereals for which limited availability or no availability is reported

children.name: Food unavailbility
% of localities where very insufficient or no food available % of localities where not enough food is available on markets

metric.label: % of localities where very insufficient or no food available % of localities where not enough food is available on markets

children.name: Access to farmland or pastures
% of localities or share of pop in a locality where access to farmlandand/or pasture is not possible or extremely limited

metric.label: % of localities or share of pop in a locality where access to farmlandand/or pasture is not possible or extremely limited

children.name: Qualitative reports on main food groups consumed
Qualitative reports on 1-2 food groups remaining (staple cereals, pulses or else) in the area, possibly only through in-kind assistance, hot meals distribution or wild foods gathering

metric.label: Qualitative reports on 1-2 food groups remaining (staple cereals, pulses or else) in the area, possibly only through in-kind assistance, hot meals distribution or wild foods gathering

children.name: Qualitative reports on inavailability of food

Qualitative reports that key food sources are depleted or extremely low (local crops destroyed, flooded, uncultivated OR markets without food), and/or alterative food sources are mainly providing irregular amounts of food to the area (wild foods, aid, etc.)

metric.label: Qualitative reports that key food sources are depleted or extremely low (local crops destroyed, flooded, uncultivated OR markets without food), and/or alterative food sources are mainly providing irregular amounts of food to the area (wild foods, aid, etc.)

children.name: Qualitative reports on depleted food stocks
Qualitative reports on food stocks (local or imported crops, warehouses stocks, etc.) at area level being depleted or (rural areas) that households' stocks are depleted (most often local crops).

metric.label: Qualitative reports on food stocks (local or imported crops, warehouses stocks, etc.) at area level being depleted or (rural areas) that households' stocks are depleted (most often local crops).

children.name: Accessibility
Main food sources
% HHs mainly relying on humanitarian assistance as food source

metric.label: % HHs mainly relying on humanitarian assistance as food source
metric.msna_indicator: % of households by main sources of food in the 30 days prior to data collection
metric.remarks_limitations:
In-kind food aid
CVA
metric.references_for_threshold: Based on WFP food source indicators and IPC analytical frameworks. No global threshold exists; ANA threshold reflects a substantial share of households dependent on external food assistance.

children.name: % HHs mainly relying on atypical food sources

metric.label: % HHs mainly relying on atypical food sources
metric.msna_indicator: % of households by main sources of food in the 30 days prior to data collection
metric.remarks_limitations:
Atypical should be defined per context. Default options include: Borrowed
Gift from family and/or friends
Begging
metric.references_for_threshold: Based on IPC and WFP coping strategy frameworks. “Atypical” food sources are context-specific and include socially or economically unsustainable sources (e.g. borrowing, begging). Threshold reflects widespread reliance on crisis coping.

children.name: Livelihoods-based coping
% of HHs reporting emergency level livelihoods based coping strategies

metric.label: % of HHs reporting emergency level livelihoods based coping strategies
metric.msna_indicator: Livelihood Coping Strategy - Essential Needs (LCS-EN)

children.name: Food prices [key commodities]
% change in local food prices against previous year and five-year average (incl. seasonality)

metric.label: % change in local food prices against previous year and five-year average (incl. seasonality)

children.name: Main sources of food (AoK)
% of localities relying mainly on humanitarian assistance and/or gathering atypically consumed wild foods

metric.label: % of localities relying mainly on humanitarian assistance and/or gathering atypically consumed wild foods
metric.references_for_threshold: No standard global threshold. Indicator used to capture severe food access constraints; threshold reflects a substantial share of affected localities.

children.name: HH purchasing power
% change in ToTs for staple foods against previous year and five-year average, incl. seasonality

metric.label: % change in ToTs for staple foods against previous year and five-year average, incl. seasonality

children.name: HEA outcome - survival deficit (households cannot meet 50% of their minimum food and non-food survival needs without irreversible coping).)

metric.label: HEA outcome - survival deficit (households cannot meet 50% of their minimum food and non-food survival needs without irreversible coping).)
metric.remarks_limitations: Survival deficit = inability to meet minimum food + non-food needs without irreversible coping
metric.references_for_threshold: Based on Household Economy Approach (HEA). Survival deficit indicates households cannot meet minimum food and non-food needs without irreversible coping.

children.name: Main source of income
% of HHs relying on atypical or"unstable" sources of income (exchange for labor, gifts, begging, etc.)

metric.label: % of HHs relying on atypical or"unstable" sources of income (exchange for labor, gifts, begging, etc.)
metric.msna_indicator: Household income over the 30 days prior to data collection, by amount and % from each source Most common sources of household income over the 30 days prior to data collection

children.name: Food prices
% of localities with a big increase in food prices in the last 30 days

metric.label: % of localities with a big increase in food prices in the last 30 days

children.name: Food access limitations (AoK)
% of localities where at least half of the pop has very limited access to food

metric.label: % of localities where at least half of the pop has very limited access to food

children.name: Coping strategies for food (AoK)
% of localities where at least half of the pop do very unusual and desperate things just to obtain a small amount of food

metric.label: % of localities where at least half of the pop do very unusual and desperate things just to obtain a small amount of food

children.name: Household food expenditures
% HH for whom food is 75% of expenditures

metric.label: % HH for whom food is 75% of expenditures
metric.msna_indicator: Household expenditures in the 30 days prior to data collection, by amount and % per type

children.name: Utilisation
Use of clean water for meal preparation
% HH using clean water to prepare food

metric.label: % HH using clean water to prepare food
metric.references_for_threshold:
Based on WHO/UNICEF guidance on safe food preparation and WASH–nutrition linkages. Threshold reflects widespread use of unsafe water for food preparation.
https://www.who.int/publications/i/item/9789241594639

children.name: Use of fuel to cook food
% HH not using fuel source to prepare food

metric.label: % HH not using fuel source to prepare food

children.name: Ability to safely store food
% HH with safe storage equipment

metric.label: % HH with safe storage equipment
metric.msna_indicator: % of households by reported food storage method

children.name: Qualitative reports of food sharing practices
Qualitative reports of HHs sharing food with other houseohlds or individuals, especially received assistance

metric.label: Qualitative reports of HHs sharing food with other houseohlds or individuals, especially received assistance

children.name: Use of surface water in food preparation
Qualitative reports of surface water use for food preparation

metric.label: Qualitative reports of surface water use for food preparation

Availability

metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metric.msna_indicator: How many {fsl_livestock_type} did you have at the same time last year? How many {fsl_livestock_type} do you have now?

% of localities where there has been a decrease in livestock (more than half at least)

children.name: Accessibility
Decrease in income
% of HHs reporting a signficant decrease in income

children.name: Qualitative reports of destitution
Qualitative reports of households without any livelihoods or income sources, without assets, and extremely limited to no external support to access income or food.

metric.label: Qualitative reports of households without any livelihoods or income sources, without assets, and extremely limited to no external support to access income or food.
metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors

children.name: Qualitative reports of zero-sum coping
Qualitative reports indicating that households members make any choice of livelihood or caregiving option leading to something else equally urgent goes unattended.

metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors

children.name: Water system
HH Water consumption
Quantity
Quantity of total water per capita
Median litres total water per person per day

metric.label: Median litres total water per person per day
metric.remarks_limitations: Low total water = signal of system stress/collapse
metric.references_for_threshold: Shpere minimum standard is 15L/person/day for all uses. ANA threshold reflects severe shorfall (<50% of minimum standard), consistent with emergency conditions

children.name: Quantity of drinking and cooking water per capita
Median litres drinking/cooking water per person per day

metric.label: Median litres drinking/cooking water per person per day
metric.remarks_limitations:
Low drinking water indicates potential survival risk
Low domestic water indicates hygiene breakdown, potential disease risk
metric.references_for_threshold: Sphere minimum standard is 6L/person/day for drinking + cooking. ANA threshold reflects severe shortfall consistent with emergency conditions.

children.name: Quantity of domestic water per capita
Median litres domestic water per person per day

metric.label: Median litres domestic water per person per day
metric.remarks_limitations: Low domestic water indicates hygiene breakdown, potential disease risk
metric.references_for_threshold: Based on Sphere: Total water (15L) - drinking/cooking (6L) leaves 9L/person/day as the domsetic threshold.

children.name: Quantity of water from programme data (water trucking)
Litres per person per day

metric.label: Litres per person per day
metric.remarks_limitations:
Low drinking water indicates potential survival risk
Low domestic water indicates hygiene breakdown, potential disease risk
metric.references_for_threshold: Based on emergency water supply practice (often 5–10 L/person/day in acute response phases); threshold reflects minimal survival-level provision.

children.name: Quality
Household water quality (E.coli contamination)
% of HHs with >100 CFU/100ml at point of use (storage container, etc.)

metric.label: % of HHs with >100 CFU/100ml at point of use (storage container, etc.)
metric.remarks_limitations: Direct exposure risk
metric.references_for_threshold:
WHO Drinking water quality guidelines. Risk categories: 0 CFU = safe, 1-10 CFU = low risk, 10-100 is intermediate risk, >100 is high risk of contamination. ANA analytical threshold of 40% suggests significant exposure. 
https://www.who.int/teams/environment-climate-change-and-health/water-sanitation-and-health/water-safety-and-quality/drinking-water-quality-guidelines

children.name: Use of unimproved drinking water sources
% of HHs relying on unimproved water sources for drinking/cooking water

metric.label: % of HHs relying on unimproved water sources for drinking/cooking water
metric.msna_indicator: % of households having had access to an improved drinking water source % of households by type of primary source of drinking water (W1)
metric.references_for_threshold:
Based on JMP service ladder (WHO/UNICEF). ANA analytical threshold indicates widespread use (>80% of HHs, systemic issue). 
https://washdata.org/topics/drinking-water

children.name: % of HHs relying on surface water as primary water source for drinking and cooking

metric.label: % of HHs relying on surface water as primary water source for drinking and cooking
metric.msna_indicator: % of households having had access to an improved drinking water source % of households by type of primary source of drinking water (W1)
metric.remarks_limitations: Reliance on surface water indicates high vulnerability, increased disease risk
metric.references_for_threshold:
Based on JMP service ladder (WHO/UNICEF). ANA analytical threshold indicates widespread use (>20% of HHs, significant share of HHs). 
https://washdata.org/topics/drinking-water

children.name: JMP ladder for drinking water unimproved or surface water
% of HHs classified as unimproved or surface water on the JMP ladder for drinking water

metric.label: % of HHs classified as unimproved or surface water on the JMP ladder for drinking water
metric.remarks_limitations: The JMP ladder is the preferred indicator to be used to inform on this sub-factor

children.name: HH Water security
Availability
HWISE-4
% HHs with HWISE-4 score >= 9

metric.label: % HHs with HWISE-4 score >= 9
metric.references_for_threshold:
Based on HWISE scale; no globally agreed severity cut-offs exist. ANA threshold reflects widespread household water insecurity (>50%). 
https://www.rural-water-supply.net/en/resources/991-

children.name: % HHs with HWISE-4 score >= 11

metric.label: % HHs with HWISE-4 score >= 11
metric.references_for_threshold:
Based on HWISE scale; no globally agreed severity cut-offs exist. ANA threshold reflects widespread household water insecurity (>50%). 
https://www.rural-water-supply.net/en/resources/991-

children.name: % HHs with HWISE-4 score >= 4

metric.label: % HHs with HWISE-4 score >= 4
metric.references_for_threshold:
Based on HWISE scale; no globally agreed severity cut-offs exist. ANA threshold reflects widespread household water insecurity (>50%). 
https://www.rural-water-supply.net/en/resources/991-

children.name: Water availability at main water source
Average number of days per week of water availability at main water source

metric.label: Average number of days per week of water availability at main water source
metric.msna_indicator: % of households by number of days water was unavailable from main source in the past month (XW8)
metric.references_for_threshold:
Sphere minimum standard reflects reliable daily access to water. ANA threshold here to indicate acutely severe availability gap. 
https://spherestandards.org/wp-content/uploads/Sphere-Handbook-2018-EN.pdf

children.name: % of households with 4 days or less per week of water availability at main water source

metric.label: % of households with 4 days or less per week of water availability at main water source
metric.msna_indicator: % of households by number of days water was unavailable from main source in the past month (XW8)
metric.remarks_limitations: Question asks about the past month but can perhaps proxy from this
metric.references_for_threshold:
Sphere minimum standard reflects reliable daily access to water. ANA threshold here to indicate significant proportion of the population affected by service gap. 
https://spherestandards.org/wp-content/uploads/Sphere-Handbook-2018-EN.pdf

children.name: Household water coping
% of HHs reporting they use less preferred, unimproved or unsafe water sources to cope with lack of drinking water

metric.label: % of HHs reporting they use less preferred, unimproved or unsafe water sources to cope with lack of drinking water
metric.msna_indicator: % of households engaging in coping mechanisms due water insufficiency, by type of coping mechanism
metric.references_for_threshold: ANA threshold, reflecting significant proportion of population using severe coping mechanism.

children.name: % of HHs reporting reducing water usage for hygiene to cope with lack of water

metric.label: % of HHs reporting reducing water usage for hygiene to cope with lack of water
metric.msna_indicator: % of households engaging in coping mechanisms due water insufficiency, by type of coping mechanism
metric.references_for_threshold: ANA threshold, reflecting majority of population using severe coping mechanism.

children.name: % of HHs reporting [other severe context specific coping] to cope with lack of water

metric.label: % of HHs reporting [other severe context specific coping] to cope with lack of water
metric.msna_indicator: % of households engaging in coping mechanisms due water insufficiency, by type of coping mechanism
metric.references_for_threshold: ANA threshold, reflecting majority of population using severe coping mechanism.

children.name: Qual reports of stressed availability of water at HH level

Indications that MOST of the popluation may be experiencing household level stress on availability of water at the household level (inadequate storage for water preventing adequate water consumption, water service interruptions, etc.)

metric.label: Indications that MOST of the popluation may be experiencing household level stress on availability of water at the household level (inadequate storage for water preventing adequate water consumption, water service interruptions, etc.)

children.name: Access
Phsyical household access to water
% of HHs with travel and queue time to water source more than 30 minutes

metric.label: % of HHs with travel and queue time to water source more than 30 minutes
metric.msna_indicator: % of households by time (minutes) taken to fetch water (round trip by walking, queuing and time needed to fetch water) (W3/W4)
metric.references_for_threshold: Sphere minimum standard = ≤30 minutes round trip. ANA threshold suggests significant share of population unable to make this roundtrip within that timeframe. https://spherestandards.org/wp-content/uploads/Sphere-Handbook-2018-EN.pdf

children.name: Financial household access to water
% of HHs spending more than 5% of income on water

metric.label: % of HHs spending more than 5% of income on water
metric.msna_indicator: Household expenditures in the 30 days prior to data collection, by amount and % per type
metric.references_for_threshold: No global threshold exists. 3-5% is often used as affordability threshold. The UN Resolution on Human Rights in Drinking Water and Sewerage (24 March 2011) sets the threshold at 5%, elsewhere 3% is reported (e.g., https://www.un.org/en/global-issues/water#:~:text=One%20of%20the%20most%20important,should%20not%20exceed%2030%20minutes.)

children.name: Household barriers to sufficient water
% of HHs reporting physical barriers to accessing water points

metric.label: % of HHs reporting physical barriers to accessing water points
metric.msna_indicator: % of households having had problems related to access to water, by type of problem

children.name: % of HHs reporting financial access barriers to accessing water points

metric.label: % of HHs reporting financial access barriers to accessing water points
metric.msna_indicator: % of households having had problems related to access to water, by type of problem

children.name: Qual reports of extreme water coping strategies

Indications that MOST of the population may be resorting to extreme coping behaviours to meet their daily water needs (spending significant daily time to fetch water each day, using unsafe or contaminated water sources, displacement due to lack of water, etc.)

metric.label: Indications that MOST of the population may be resorting to extreme coping behaviours to meet their daily water needs (spending significant daily time to fetch water each day, using unsafe or contaminated water sources, displacement due to lack of water, etc.)

children.name: Qual reports of limited physical access to water

Indications that MOST of the popluation may be facing serious physical access constraints to accessing their main drinking water sources (insecurity or lack of safety on the way to or at the water point, flooding or other natural hazard cutting off access, etc.)

metric.label: Indications that MOST of the popluation may be facing serious physical access constraints to accessing their main drinking water sources (insecurity or lack of safety on the way to or at the water point, flooding or other natural hazard cutting off access, etc.)

children.name: Qual reports of limited financial access to water

Indications that some of the popluation may be facing serious financial access constraints to accessing their main drinking water sources (extreme price increases double or triple the normal price, selling of assets to afford water, etc.)

metric.label: Indications that some of the popluation may be facing serious financial access constraints to accessing their main drinking water sources (extreme price increases double or triple the normal price, selling of assets to afford water, etc.)

children.name: Utilisation
Water storage
% of households with at least two water containers (one for storage and one for collection)

metric.label: % of households with at least two water containers (one for storage and one for collection)
metric.references_for_threshold: No global threshold, often referenced in WASH programming guidance.

children.name: Cost of water containers, treatment materials, or other items related to water utilization in the market
% change in price of water containers

metric.label: % change in price of water containers

children.name: % change in water treatment materials

metric.label: % change in water treatment materials

children.name: Water containers not available in market

metric.label: Water containers not available in market

children.name: Water treatment materials not available in market

metric.label: Water treatment materials not available in market

children.name: Qual reports of struggles with water treatment

Indications that MOST of the popluation may be facing serious challenges with water utilization due to lack of options to treat water (drinking unsafe water directly, lack of fuel to boil water, etc.) and that it is putting them at heightened risk for water borne disease transmission

metric.label: Indications that MOST of the popluation may be facing serious challenges with water utilization due to lack of options to treat water (drinking unsafe water directly, lack of fuel to boil water, etc.) and that it is putting them at heightened risk for water borne disease transmission

children.name: Qual reports of struggles with water storage

Indications that MOST of the popluation may be facing serious challenges with water utilization due to lack of water storage capacity (water consumption and use limited by number of containers, utilizing unclean/contaminated or broken water containers, lack of water containers in the market, etc.) and that it is putting them at heightened risk for water borne disease transmission

metric.label: Indications that MOST of the popluation may be facing serious challenges with water utilization due to lack of water storage capacity (water consumption and use limited by number of containers, utilizing unclean/contaminated or broken water containers, lack of water containers in the market, etc.) and that it is putting them at heightened risk for water borne disease transmission

children.name: Consumption
Qual reports of populations struggling to meet water needs

Indications that most of the population is struggling to access their minimum water needs. (e.g. reported long queues, fighting at water points, spikes in drinking water prices, use of livelihood coping strategies to buy water)

metric.label: Indications that most of the population is struggling to access their minimum water needs. (e.g. reported long queues, fighting at water points, spikes in drinking water prices, use of livelihood coping strategies to buy water)

children.name: Quality
Qual reports of poor water quality

Indications that MOST of the population may be reliant on drinking water sources that are exposed to some contamination (e.g. sources of contamination near or at water point including human or animal feces, dead animals, trash or other solid waste, flood waters or other standing/stangant water, etc.)

metric.label: Indications that MOST of the population may be reliant on drinking water sources that are exposed to some contamination (e.g. sources of contamination near or at water point including human or animal feces, dead animals, trash or other solid waste, flood waters or other standing/stangant water, etc.)

children.name: Living conditions
Living conditions
Exposure to elements
Adequate shelter
% of HHs with inadequate shelter

metric.label: % of HHs with inadequate shelter
metric.msna_indicator: % of households by type of shelter they currently live in
metric.remarks_limitations: Check contextualized options for inadequate shelter type
metric.references_for_threshold: Based on Sphere shelter standards (shelter must provide protection from climate, safety, and privacy). Threshold reflects a substantial share of households lacking adequate protection from environmental conditions; definition of inadequacy is context-specific.

children.name: Cost of tarpaulin or other key shelter materials
% change in tarpaulin or other shelter materials

metric.label: % change in tarpaulin or other shelter materials

children.name: No tarpaulin (or other key shelter material) available in market

metric.label: No tarpaulin (or other key shelter material) available in market

children.name: Qualitative reports of inadequate shelter

Indications that MOST of the population may be living in inadequate shelter that either (a) is not durable or sturdy (b) does not provide adequate privacy (c) does not provide safety or protection from the weather/elements (d) does not provide safe place to sleep

metric.label: Indications that MOST of the population may be living in inadequate shelter that either (a) is not durable or sturdy (b) does not provide adequate privacy (c) does not provide safety or protection from the weather/elements (d) does not provide safe place to sleep

% of HHs relying on unimproved sanitation and living in inadequate shelters

% of households by type of shelter they currently live in

children.name: Protection from vectors
Mosquito exposure
% of people sleeping under an insecticide treated bed net the previous night

metric.label: % of people sleeping under an insecticide treated bed net the previous night

children.name: Cost of mosquito nets
% change in cost of mosquito nets

metric.label: % change in cost of mosquito nets

children.name: No mosquito nets available in market

metric.label: No mosquito nets available in market

children.name: Qualitative reports of exposure to disease vectors

Indications that some of the population may have increased exposure to disease vectors due to inadequate shelter or the environment. This may include: (a) standing water as mosquito breeding grounds (b) poor ventilation of shelters(c) contamined or waste water near shelter(d) rats, pests, or other disease carriers

metric.label: Indications that some of the population may have increased exposure to disease vectors due to inadequate shelter or the environment. This may include: (a) standing water as mosquito breeding grounds (b) poor ventilation of shelters(c) contamined or waste water near shelter(d) rats, pests, or other disease carriers

children.name: Qualitative reports of overcrowding

Indications that MOST of the population may be overcrowded and may be facing increased risks for respiratory disease, protection concerns, or mental health distress. Some indications may include:(a) multiple households sharing shelter (b) people cramped into small shared sleeping spaces(c) not all household members can lie down or fit in the sleeping space(d) reports of widespread coughing or respiratory issues (e) reports of tensions or fighting between neighbours due to proximity and lack of privacy (f) long lines or waits for communal facilities such as latrines, water, etc.

metric.label: Indications that MOST of the population may be overcrowded and may be facing increased risks for respiratory disease, protection concerns, or mental health distress. Some indications may include:(a) multiple households sharing shelter (b) people cramped into small shared sleeping spaces(c) not all household members can lie down or fit in the sleeping space(d) reports of widespread coughing or respiratory issues (e) reports of tensions or fighting between neighbours due to proximity and lack of privacy (f) long lines or waits for communal facilities such as latrines, water, etc.

children.name: Overcrowding
Adequate living space
Average covered living space per person

metric.label: Average covered living space per person
metric.references_for_threshold: Based on Sphere minimum standards: Minimum 3.5 m2 of living space per person, excluding cooking space, bathing area and sanitation facility. https://spherestandards.org/wp-content/uploads/Sphere-in-Practice.pdf

children.name: % of HHs with at least 3.5 m2 of covered living space per person

metric.label: % of HHs with at least 3.5 m2 of covered living space per person
metric.references_for_threshold: Based on Sphere minimum standards: Minimum 3.5 m2 of living space per person, ANA threshold suggests significant population facing gap.

children.name: Population density estimated through satellite imagery
Estimated people per square metre

metric.label: Estimated people per square metre
metric.references_for_threshold: No standard humanitarian threshold; proxy indicator derived from population density estimates to identify high-risk overcrowding.

children.name: Environmental Hygiene
Exposure to elements
Environmental sanitation problems
% of HHs having faced environmental sanitation problems (living in areas where solid waste, waterwaste, open defecation were visible around their accommodation - 30 meters or less)

metric.label: % of HHs having faced environmental sanitation problems (living in areas where solid waste, waterwaste, open defecation were visible around their accommodation - 30 meters or less)
metric.msna_indicator: Environmental threaths in the environment
metric.references_for_threshold:
Based on Sphere sanitation standards regarding safe waste management and environmental health. Threshold reflects widespread exposure to unsanitary living environments.
https://spherestandards.org/wp-content/uploads/Sphere-in-Practice.pdf

children.name: Sanitation & Hygiene
Availability
Sufficient number of latrines
# of people per latrine

metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metric.msna_indicator: % of households sharing their sanitation facility, by number of households per sanitation facility (S2)
metric.remarks_limitations: Calculate using humind and average household size
metric.references_for_threshold:
Sphere minimum standard (emergency target) = ≤20 people per latrine. Threshold reflects severe shortage consistent with emergency conditions.
https://spherestandards.org/wp-content/uploads/Sphere-in-Practice.pdf

children.name: Health/Nutrition services
Health services
Availability
Health facilities per population
Number of people per primary health facility catchment area

metric.label: Number of people per primary health facility catchment area
metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metric.remarks_limitations: Right to health imposes four essential standards on healthcare services: Availabiltiy, Accessibility, Acceptability, and Quality (AAAQ), from WHO. Indicators used here reflect this framework. https://www.who.int/news-room/questions-and-answers/item/q-a-on-the-health-workforce-crisis#:~:text=What%20do%20we%20mean%20by,outcomes%20will%20not%20be%20satisfactory.
metric.references_for_threshold:
WHO and national planning norms often benchmark primary care facility catchment as 10,000 - 20,000 per PHC. UNHCR Emergency Handbook describes 10,000 people per basic health unit in the emergency phase. 
https://www.who.int/publications/i/item/9789240044210 
https://emergency.unhcr.org/emergency-assistance/health-and-nutrition/primary-health-care/primary-health-care-coverage-standard

children.name: Number of people per secondary health facility (hospitals)

metric.label: Number of people per secondary health facility (hospitals)
metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors

children.name: Number of inpatient beds per 10,000 people

metric.label: Number of inpatient beds per 10,000 people
metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metric.references_for_threshold:
Sphere handbook specifies a minimum of 18 beds / 10,0000 persons (excluding maternity beds) to ensure adequate coverage during emergencies. ANA threshold indicates severe gap. 
http://www.humanitarianstandardspartnership.org/ViewContent?DocID=1000011&VersionID=2000017&ChapterNumber=8&OrderInChapter=6&Lang=en

children.name: Functioning health facilities
% of functional primary health care (PHC) facilities

metric.label: % of functional primary health care (PHC) facilities
metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metric.references_for_threshold: No gblobal thresholds exist, "functionality* states can be based on WHO HeRAMS or other health facility monitoring. ANA threshold reflects functionality levels consistent with major health system disruption.

metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metric.references_for_threshold: No gblobal thresholds exist, "functionality* states can be based on WHO HeRAMS or other health facility monitoring. ANA threshold reflects functionality levels consistent with major health system disruption.

Based on WHO and UNICEF frameworks for skilled birth attendance and maternal health workforce capacity. No global density thresholds exist; ANA thresholds reflect severe constraints in availability of skilled maternal health personnel.

metric.msna_indicator: Household expenditures in the 30 days prior to data collection, by amount and % per type
metric.remarks_limitations: Probably more of an infrequent expenditure situation?

children.name: Quality
Case fatality rates (disease specific)
% Measles case fatality rates

metric.label: % Measles case fatality rates
metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metric.references_for_threshold:
Current estimates used by WHO for CFRs in endemic countries range between 0.05-6%. ANA threshold is set at 5% as a signal of concern. 
https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19188207/

children.name: % Cholera case fatality rates

metric.label: % Cholera case fatality rates
metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metric.references_for_threshold: WHO handles an emergency threshold of >1% CFR for Cholera in treatment centres. According to the Global Taskforce on Cholera Control, a CFR >1% is usually due to one or a combination of different factors, such as poor access to health facilities, lack of healthcare-seeking behaviour, and/or inadequate case management. https://www.gtfcc.org/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/gtfcc-cholera-outbreak-response-field-manual-2024.pdf

children.name: % Ebola case fatality rates

metric.label: % Ebola case fatality rates
metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metric.references_for_threshold:
Ebola has a high baseline CFR, estimations of the Ebola CFR from the years 1976-2022 approach around 60% globally. ANA threshold set at the higher end of baseline estimates. 
https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1876034123003581

children.name: Minimum package of services
% of health facilities providing minimum package of primary health services as defined by in country policies

metric.label: % of health facilities providing minimum package of primary health services as defined by in country policies
metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metric.references_for_threshold:
Based on WHO essential health service frameworks. Threshold reflects reduced availability of core health services.
https://www.who.int/data/gho/indicator-metadata-registry/imr-details/7793#:~:text=IV.&text=Associated%20terms:,and%20the%20most%20disadvantaged%20population.&text=Method%20of%20estimation:,to%20100%20(optimal%20value).&text=Step%203:%20Calculate%20the%20geometric,values%20calculated%20in%20Step%202.

children.name: % of women of reproductive age (15-49 years) with a live birth in the last two years who during the most recent live birth were attended at least once by a skilled health care worker (doctor, nurse, or midwife)

metric.label: % of women of reproductive age (15-49 years) with a live birth in the last two years who during the most recent live birth were attended at least once by a skilled health care worker (doctor, nurse, or midwife)
metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metric.msna_indicator: % of women of reproductive age (15-49 years) with a live birth in the last two years who during the most recent live birth were attended at least once by a skilled health personnel
metric.references_for_threshold: Based on WHO/UNICEF indicator for births attended by skilled health personnel (SDG 3.1.2). No universally agreed humanitarian severity thresholds exist; ANA thresholds reflect levels associated with severe constraints in maternal and newborn health service access.

children.name: Nutrition services
Quality
Quality of SAM Nutrition Services- CMAM performance: (Cure rates, Defaulter rates, Death rates)
CMAM performance - cure rates-SAM %

metric.label: CMAM performance - cure rates-SAM %
metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metric.references_for_threshold: Based on SQUEAC/SLEAC (Action Against Hunger / Valid International) coverage standards, and Nutrition Working group: Technical Issue Paper #1 CMAM - specific programme indicators: >75% Cure rate. ANA thresholds reflect insufficient coverage to meet population needs. https://ec.europa.eu/echo/files/policies/sectoral/TIP%20CMAM.pdf

children.name: CMAM performance - death rates-SAM %

metric.label: CMAM performance - death rates-SAM %
metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metric.references_for_threshold: Based on SQUEAC/SLEAC (Action Against Hunger / Valid International) coverage standards, and Nutrition Working group: Technical Issue Paper #1 CMAM - specific programme indicator: >10% death rate. ANA thresholds reflect insufficient coverage to meet population needs.  https://ec.europa.eu/echo/files/policies/sectoral/TIP%20CMAM.pdf

children.name: CMAM performance - defaulter rates- SAM %

metric.label: CMAM performance - defaulter rates- SAM %
metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metric.references_for_threshold: ased on SQUEAC/SLEAC (Action Against Hunger / Valid International) coverage standards, and Nutrition Working group: Technical Issue Paper #1 CMAM - specific programme indicators: >15% defaulters. ANA thresholds reflect insufficient coverage to meet population needs. https://ec.europa.eu/echo/files/policies/sectoral/TIP%20CMAM.pdf

metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metric.references_for_threshold: Less standardised for MAM compared to SAM, threshold reflects similar expectations (see above)

children.name: Accessibility & Availability (Coverage of SC, OTP or TSFP nutrition services.)
Coverage of SC/ OTP/ SFP in IDP camps

metric.label: Coverage of SC/ OTP/ SFP in IDP camps
metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metric.references_for_threshold:
Sphere minimum standards for perforamnce indicators of coverage: in camp settings suggests >90% coverage as acceptable access. ANA threshold reflects severe access gap. 
https://www.indikit.net/document/17-standardised-indicators-and-categories-for-better-cmam-reporting#:~:text=This%20paper%20outlines%20standardised%20reporting,systems%20to%20fit%20best%20practice.

children.name: Coverage of SC/ OTP/ SFP in urban populations

metric.label: Coverage of SC/ OTP/ SFP in urban populations
metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metric.references_for_threshold: Sphere minimum standards for perforamnce indicators of coverage: in camp settings suggests >70% coverage as acceptable access in urban settings. ANA threshold reflects severe access gap. https://www.indikit.net/document/17-standardised-indicators-and-categories-for-better-cmam-reporting#:~:text=This%20paper%20outlines%20standardised%20reporting,systems%20to%20fit%20best%20practice.

children.name: Coverage of SC/ OTP/ SFP in rural populations

metric.label: Coverage of SC/ OTP/ SFP in rural populations
metric.level: Community or system level contributing factors
metric.references_for_threshold: Sphere minimum standards for perforamnce indicators of coverage: in camp settings suggests >50% coverage as acceptable access in rural settings. ANA threshold reflects severe access gap. https://www.indikit.net/document/17-standardised-indicators-and-categories-for-better-cmam-reporting#:~:text=This%20paper%20outlines%20standardised%20reporting,systems%20to%20fit%20best%20practice.
